data.content:
## Implementation Plan: Daily updater notices uncommitted managed skill updates

### Problem
The daily update workflow runs `kno skills update <tool>`, which rewrites managed SKILL.md files under the repo (all managed destinations are Project scope: repo_root/.claude/skills and repo_root/.agents/skills — see managed_skills.rs:111-132). `write_skills` (managed_skills_ops.rs:146-164) unconditionally rewrites every installed skill, so the existing 'updated N managed skill(s)' output (managed_skills_output.rs:20-33) can leave tracked files modified vs HEAD with no nudge to commit. Observed: .agents/skills/knots-e2e/SKILL.md and .claude/skills/knots-e2e/SKILL.md left dirty.

### Goal / Acceptance
After managed skill updates modify tracked files, `update_managed` appends a clear trailing notice listing the changed files and telling the user to commit them. No notice when nothing tracked actually changed; no error when not in a git repo.

### Design
Detection signal = git, not write_skills' return (which over-reports since it always rewrites). After writing, ask git which of the written paths are actually dirty/tracked.

1. New module src/managed_skills_git.rs (keeps ops.rs well under 500 lines):
   - `pub(super) fn changed_tracked_paths(repo_root: &Path, paths: &[PathBuf]) -> Vec<PathBuf>`
   - Shells out: `git -C <repo_root> status --porcelain -- <paths...>` (pattern mirrors sync/git.rs:47-57 status_clean).
   - Parses porcelain lines, returns the subset of input paths reported as changed (modified/added/untracked-but-tracked-parent). Returns empty Vec on any git failure (not a repo, git missing) — graceful, never errors. Register module via #[path] in managed_skills.rs (alongside ops/state/output, lines 8-30).

2. managed_skills_output.rs — add `pub(super) fn format_commit_notice(repo_root: &Path, paths: &[PathBuf]) -> String`:
   - Returns a trailing block, e.g.:
     'Note: managed skill updates modified tracked files in <repo_root>. Commit them:\n- <path>\n...'
   - Only produces text; caller decides whether to append.

3. managed_skills_ops.rs update_managed (lines 51-83) — after `write_skills` + `finalize_install`:
   - `let dirty = changed_tracked_paths(repo_root, &updated_paths);`
   - If non-empty, append '\n\n' + format_commit_notice(repo_root, &dirty) to the returned output string.
   - Keep base output (format_changed_paths) unchanged. Scope to the update path only (matches the daily-updater scenario and acceptance). Note: install_missing could adopt the same helper later — out of scope here.

### Test Strategy (managed_skills_tests_ext.rs — git-aware; uses real git repos via init_git_repo helper, lines 22-34)
- POSITIVE: init repo, install skills, commit a STALE version of one SKILL.md, run update -> canonical rewrite differs from HEAD -> git reports modified -> assert output contains the path and a 'commit' instruction.
- NEGATIVE (clean): install skills, git add+commit canonical content, run update -> identical rewrite -> git clean -> assert NO commit notice in output.
- NON-REPO: run update in a destination whose repo_root is not a git repo -> changed_tracked_paths returns empty -> no notice, no error.
- Unit-level: format_commit_notice formatting (path list + repo_root) and changed_tracked_paths porcelain parsing if feasible without a repo.

### Files
- NEW: src/managed_skills_git.rs (~40-60 lines)
- EDIT: src/managed_skills.rs (#[path] module registration + use)
- EDIT: src/managed_skills_ops.rs (call site in update_managed)
- EDIT: src/managed_skills_output.rs (format_commit_notice)
- EDIT: src/managed_skills_tests_ext.rs (new tests)

### Constraints / Risks
- File length <500, function body <100, line width <100 — all new code small; respected.
- Coverage >=95%: cover changed/unchanged/non-repo branches + formatter. 
- git absent or non-repo must degrade silently (return empty, no AppError).
- One extra `git status` call per update — negligible cost.
- No invariants on this knot.
- make sanity (fmt, lint, tests, coverage) must pass before merge.
